data.title: Treasury Yields March Higher on Tariffs Concerns
data.description:
The yield on the US 10-year Treasury note rose about 6 bps to 4.39% on Thursday, its highest level in nearly a month, as traders assessed the escalating trade war and its potential economic impact.

The Trump administration announced a 25% tariff on "all cars not made in the United States," set to take effect on April 2, while President Trump warned of imposing "far larger" tariffs on the EU and Canada if they coordinate efforts to counter US trade measures.

Concerns are mounting that these tariffs could hurt the US economy, particularly as key economic indicators signal potential trouble.

The latest CB Consumer Confidence report showed a decline in March, reaching its lowest level in four years.

Additionally, orders for non-defense capital goods excluding aircraft fell 0.3%, missing expectations of a 0.2% increase.

Investors now await the final estimate for Q4 GDP growth and the latest jobless claims report, both due today, while the PCE report is scheduled for release tomorrow.

data.title: Signify, Dixon Technologies Plan Lighting Products, Accessories JV in India
data.description:
Signify said Thursday it plans to set up a 50/50 joint venture with Indian electronics manufacturer Dixon Technologies, subject to regulatory approvals.

The Dutch lighting company said the venture will produce lighting products and accessories for brands in India.

data.title: REG-AKVA group ASA: Sale of shares in Abyss Group to Arcus Infrastructure Partners
data.description:
Reference is made to the stock exchange notice on 12 March 2025 where AKVA group ASA (“AKVA”) announced the sale of its 21,55% ownership in Abyss Group AS to Arcus Infrastructure Partners LLP.

The transaction was closed today. AKVA's net proceeds from the sale is approx. MNOK 140 and will result in a positive accounting gain which will be recognized in Q1 2025. The proceeds from the transaction will be used for continued development of AKVA’s core business segments.

Dated: 27 March 2025

AKVA group ASA

Web: www.akvagroup.com

CONTACTS:

Knut Nesse Chief Executive Officer
Phone:+47 51 77 85 00
Mobile:+47 91 37 62 20
E-mail:knesse@akvagroup.com
Ronny MeinkøhnChief Financial Officer
Phone:+47 51 77 85 00
Mobile:+47 98 20 67 76
E-mail:rmeinkohn@akvagroup.com

***

This information is considered to be inside information pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation and is subject to the disclosure requirements according to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act. The information was submitted for publication by Ronny Meinkøhn (CFO) (contact details as set out above) on 27 March 2025 at 12:10 CET.

About AKVA group ASA

AKVA group ASA is a global technology and service partner that deliver technology and services  that helps  solve  biological  challenges within the aquaculture industry.  Good operational performance and fish welfare ensures sustainability and profitability for the customer.  This is the premise for everything we deliver, from single components to services and complete installations. In-depth aquaculture knowledge, extensive experience and a high capacity for innovation characterizes and enables us to deliver the best solutions for both land based and sea based fish farming.

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Thursday economic news summary pointed out:

— Euro Stoxx -0.9%, 10-year Bund -4bps at 2.75%, 10-year United States Treasury +4bps at 4.39%. defends 200dma (1.0733). European Central Bank rate cut in April likely after the U.S. announces 25% tariffs on imports of all assembled cars and components effective next Wednesday. Reciprocal tariffs on all nations next week to be "very lenient." European trade commissioner Sefcovic expects tariffs of around 20% applicable equally to all 27 member states.

— Norway's Norges Bank leaves rate on hold at 4.50%, signals cut later this year, raises rate forecast through 2027 on wages and inflation.

— Bernstein sector notes: European Union Autos: Punched by the Tariff Man. U.S. Autos: 25% tariffs on all vehicle imports deals blow to sector.

— Japanese funds sold net 207 billion yen international bonds and bought 272.1 billion yen international stocks last week. International funds sold net 1.78 billion yen Japanese bonds and 1.2 billion yen stocks.

— Day ahead: U.S. weekly jobless claims. Fed speakers Barkin and Collins. European Central Bank's Guindos, Villeroy, Wunsch, Escriva and Schnabel. Bank of England's Dhingra. Mexico's Banxico forecasts to cut by 50bps.

— Nikkei -0.6%, EUR 10-year IRS +2bps at 2.70%, Brent crude -0.3% at $73.5/barrel, Gold +0.4% at $3,031/oz.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1105 GMT - MFE-MediaForEurope's bid to raise its ProsiebenSat.1 stake is a strategic move aimed at avoiding a more expensive mandatory takeover offer later, Warburg analyst Joerg Philipp Frey says in a note. MFE has steadily increased its ownership to just below 30%, a threshold that would trigger a mandatory takeover offer under German market rules. The European media group's takeover offer for ProsiebenSat.1 is likely to see low uptake as the proposal is unattractive for most investors, he says. The low offer price--representing a 12% discount to Wednesday's closing price--and the share-based components are key deterrents, Frey says. Shares of ProSiebenSat.1 fall 11% to 5.82 euros, while MFE shares are down 1.7% at 4.74 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1059 GMT - Renk Group's midterm targets remain unchanged, but Germany's push to rearm is likely to become visible in the company's financials after 2027, Warburg analyst Christian Cohrs says. The German defense company's revenue could rise to 3.4 billion euros in 2033, according to Warburg's estimates. The group's earnings for 2024 didn't contain surprises, but it is well positioned for this year, Cohrs says. Renk is targeting about 15% revenue growth to more than 1.3 billion euros. Due to some fine-tuning of operational forecasts, Warburg cuts its rating for Renk stock to hold from buy, and raises its price target to 41.5 euros from 36 euros. Shares are down 1.2% at 45.11 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

1050 GMT - Repsol's joint venture with NEO Energy makes sense given the U.K. government's fiscal policy on oil and gas developments, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. The joint venture continues a trend seen across U.K. North Sea exposed companies because it allows operators to pool resources and allocate less capital in the region while continuing to grow, they write. Repsol's 2024 capital expenditure in the U.K. was around 200 million euros and the entity is expected to be de-consolidated on the deal closing, the analysts write. Shares trade up 0.7% at 12.50 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1048 GMT - The Canadian dollar falls only slightly after U.S. President Trump announced a 25% tariff on global automotive imports into the U.S. This suggests the currency has already priced in much of the concerns and uncertainty surrounding tariffs, Societe Generale's Kit Juckes says in a note. Trump's latest announcement dampens hopes that tariff threats could ease off. "It's worth noting that the latest tariff outburst has had only a very small impact on the Canadian dollar, which continues to give the impression of having priced in a lot of the tariff news," he says. Canada is the fourth top exporter of cars to the U.S., according to S&P Global Mobility. The U.S. dollar rises 0.2% to 1.4293 Canadian dollars. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

1040 GMT - Indian shares closed higher, supported by bank and finance stocks. Investors are watching for further developments on Trump's trade policies, especially ahead of the announcement of worldwide reciprocal tariffs expected to take place on April 2. Financial stocks led gains, with Bajaj Finserv up 3.2% and Indusind Bank up 2.7%. Bajaj Finance rose 1.4% and State Bank of India gained 1.2%. Among decliners, Tata Motors led the losses, down 5.6% and Mahindra & Mahindra was down 0.35%. Tata Steel lost 0.3%. The benchmark Sensex closed 0.4% higher at 77606.43. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

1039 GMT - Next PLC's guidance upgrade is unexpected this early in the year, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a research note. The U.K. retail bellwether raised its profit and sales estimates for fiscal year 2026 after it reported "another strong set of results," according to DB. "Given the economic uncertainty and volatility we are hearing elsewhere, this is a real sign of confidence from Next," DB says. The raised outlook, coupled with better expectations for the first six months and the potential of improvement in the second half, are a positive surprise, the analysts say. Shares are up 6% at 105.85 pounds. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1022 GMT - MFE-MediaForEurope's takeover bid for ProSiebenSat.1 allows the European media group to gain control while delaying a potentially more expensive acquisition until after ProSiebenSat.1 sells its non-core assets, AlphaValue analyst Jean-Michel Salvador says in a note. However, the offer is unfavourable to the German media conglomerate's minority shareholders, priced around 5.75 euros per share, significantly below yesterday's closing price of 6.50 euros, he says. "It is uncertain whether ProSieben shareholders will really benefit from the sale proceeds while being left with a depleted ProSieben that MFE may not acquire at a premium," the analyst says. Shares of ProSiebenSat.1 fall 11% to 5.82 euros, while MFE shares are down 1.7% at 4.74 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1013 GMT - Germany's defense budget should be invested in the domestic defense industry, Hensoldt's Chief Executive Oliver Dorre says. The country's lawmakers recently voted to exempt defense spending from Germany's strict debt rules, and create an infrastructure fund worth 500 billion euros. In Hensoldt's annual report, Dorre says the German industry can only plan for the long term if there are multi-year funding commitments. "German budget funds are therefore better invested in Germany. Equally important is a reliable export policy. Every euro that other countries spend on defence goods 'Made in Germany' flows back into our economy and strengthens our capacity for innovation," he writes. Shares are down 1.6% at 67.95 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

1001 GMT - The 25% tariffs on auto imports into the U.S. announced by President Trump on Wednesday could severely impact the European and U.K. automotive industries, Quilter investment strategist Lindsay James says in a note. The U.S. accounts for around 18% of U.K. auto exports and over 20% of eurozone exports, James says. Additionally, the auto industry contributes 7% of GDP and 6% of employment in the eurozone. "There will be an obvious blowback on a sector that has already faced considerable headwinds from a painful transition to EVs alongside tighter regulations, in addition to slower replacement cycles from drivers and higher energy costs," she says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0956 GMT - H&M consensus earnings expectations for this year could be downgraded by at least a mid single digit percentage after the company reported a meaningful first quarter EBIT miss, Morgan Stanley analysts write. EBIT of 1.2 billion Swedish kronor is well below the consensus forecast of 1.85 billion kronor, primarily driven by weaker than expected gross margin. Looking ahead, H&M expects the year-on-year gross-margin pressure to moderate in the second quarter and seemingly inflect to tailwinds in the second half. The bank sees downside risk to the second half gross margin inflection. H&M expects March sales growth of 1%, which is below consensus of 2% for the second quarter as a whole, with a tougher comparison in April and May still to come. Shares fall 0.7%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0953 GMT - Jungheinrich has set ambitious targets for 2025, which imply 9% order growth and 6% revenue growth at the mid-point, Jefferies analysts write in a note to clients. After a challenging macroeconomic environment in 2024, the targets for next year signal a decent recovery in Europe and the U.S. The German manufacturer of lifting and stacking products also published fourth-quarter order, revenue and earnings before interest and taxes in line with consensus expectations, they add. Last year's cost-cutting measures and reduced workforce clearly benefit the company's margins, they add. Shares trade up 4.7% at 35.34 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0943 GMT - Singapore shares ended higher, supported by industrial stocks. The benchmark FTSE Straits Times Index rose 0.45% to 3981.57. Jardine Matheson Holdings gained 1.8% and ST Engineering was 0.9% higher. DFI Retail Group was the best performer on the index, rising 2.1%. Among the top losers, Singapore Airlines dropped 0.6% and Genting Singapore was 0.7% lower. Citi analysts think Singapore remains the most defensive market in Asean due to its sustained currency outperformance and better dividend yield proposition, among other reasons. Citi says these factors should support the market against macro headwinds, although upside will likely remain challenging. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: BeyondSpring Files 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K
data.description:
FLORHAM PARK, N.J., March 27, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — BeyondSpring Inc. (“BeyondSpring” or the “Company”), a global clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing innovative cancer therapies, today announced that it has filed its annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”) on March 27, 2025. The annual report on Form 10-K, which contains the Company’s audited consolidated financial statements, can be accessed on the SEC’s website at www.sec.gov and on the Company’s website at www.beyondspringpharma.com under “Financials & Filings” in the Investors section.

The Company will provide a hard copy of its annual report containing its audited consolidated financial statements, free of charge, to its shareholders upon request. Requests should be directed to Investor Relations, BeyondSpring Inc., 100 Campus Drive, Suite 410, Florham Park, NJ 07932 USA.

About BeyondSpring

BeyondSpring is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing first-in-class therapies for high unmet medical needs. Its lead asset, Plinabulin, is in late-stage clinical development as an anti-cancer agent in NSCLC and a range of cancer indications. Plinabulin’s novel mechanism of action as a dendritic cell maturation agent supports both anti-cancer activity and immune modulation, offering a unique approach to resensitizing tumors to checkpoint inhibitors. Learn more at beyondspringpharma.com.

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

This press release includes forward-looking statements that are not historical facts. Words such as “will,” “expect,” “anticipate,” “plan,” “believe,” “design,” “may,” “future,” “estimate,” “predict,” “objective,” “goal,” or variations thereof and variations of such words and similar expressions are intended to identify such forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are based on BeyondSpring’s current knowledge and its present beliefs and expectations regarding possible future events and are subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions. Actual results and the timing of events could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements as a result of several factors including, but not limited to, difficulties raising the anticipated amount needed to finance the Company’s future operations on terms acceptable to the Company, if at all, unexpected results of clinical trials, delays or denial in regulatory approval process, results that do not meet the Company’s expectations regarding the potential safety, the ultimate efficacy or clinical utility of the Company’s product candidates, increased competition in the market, the Company’s ability to meet Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements, and other risks described in BeyondSpring’s most recent Form 10-K on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Methanol prices remain flat in the United States
data.description: Despite higher upstream crude values, methanol prices were assessed stable in the US on Wednesday.An industry source in the US informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Spot methanol prices held steady amid a lack of bids and offers."Methanol prices were assessed at the 95.00-95.50 cents/gallon FOB US Gulf mark, steady from Tuesday's assessed levels.Meanwhile in Asia, methanol prices on Wednesday were assessed at the USD 300-302/mt CFR China levels, a day on day drop of USD (-1/mt).
